_comment: Travel map destinations. Camp Chilly Wa Wa is intentionally omitted per the retro Hokus Pokus Pink reference. `facts` replaces the single `info` string: each entry becomes a paragraph in the info panel. `info` is still honored for backwards compat. `relevantWhen` gates bright-gold glow on the map (see game/npc_rules.go:evalCondition).

locations.name: Paris
facts: The City of Lights, capital of France.
Home of the Eiffel Tower (1889) - 300m of wrought iron built for the World's Fair.
The Louvre holds 380,000 artworks, including Leonardo's Mona Lisa.

locations.name: Jerusalem
facts: One of the oldest cities in the world, in modern-day Israel.
The Western Wall is the last remaining piece of the Second Temple (70 AD).
Ancient tunnels run beneath the Old City's stone streets.

locations.name: Tokyo
facts: Capital of Japan, a city of ancient temples and modern towers.
Senso-ji in Asakusa is Tokyo's oldest temple, founded in 645 AD.
Cherry blossoms bloom every spring - the sakura festival draws millions.

locations.name: Rome
facts: The Eternal City, capital of Italy.
The Colosseum (72 AD) seated 50,000 spectators for gladiator games.
Throw a coin in the Trevi Fountain to ensure you'll return to Rome.

locations.name: Rio de Janeiro
facts: Brazil's famous coastal city, framed by mountains and beaches.
Christ the Redeemer stands 38m tall atop Corcovado Mountain.
Carnival draws over 2 million people to Rio's streets each year.

locations.name: Buenos Aires
facts: Capital of Argentina, famous for tango dance and music.
The Obelisco marks the heart of Avenida 9 de Julio, the world's widest avenue.
La Boca's colorful houses were painted with leftover ship paint by dockworkers.

locations.name: Mexico City
facts: Ancient capital of the Aztec Empire, built on a lake.
Teotihuacan's Pyramid of the Sun rises 65m above the valley.
Frida Kahlo's Casa Azul in Coyoacan inspired generations of artists.

locations.name: Egypt
facts: Ancient cradle of civilization along the Nile River.
The Great Pyramid of Giza was the tallest man-made structure for 3,800 years.
The Sphinx has watched over the pyramids for 4,500 years.

locations.name: India
facts: Land of ancient temples, spices, and over 1.4 billion people.
The Taj Mahal (1653) was built by Emperor Shah Jahan for his late wife.
Jaipur's Hawa Mahal has 953 windows so royal women could watch the street unseen.

locations.name: Thailand
facts: The Land of Smiles, in Southeast Asia.
Wat Pho in Bangkok houses a 46m-long reclining golden Buddha.
Floating markets on Bangkok's canals sell mangoes, pad thai, and lotus flowers.

locations.name: China
facts: One of the world's oldest continuous civilizations.
The Great Wall stretches over 13,000 miles, built over 2,000 years.
The Forbidden City in Beijing has 9,999 rooms - emperors believed 10,000 was heaven-only.

locations.name: Australia
facts: The island continent in the Southern Hemisphere.
The Sydney Opera House opened in 1973 - its sails were inspired by orange segments.
The Great Barrier Reef is the only living thing on Earth visible from space.
